summary:
The key point is distinguishing between vessel size involved—large vs. small—and their associated manifestations. Giant cell arteritis affects only large arterial vessels such as the temporal artery, so it lacks the characteristic feature of palpable purpura caused by inflamed small blood vessels found in diseases like Henoch-Schönlein purpura.  

Therefore, palpatible purpura is NOT typically seen in giant cell arteritis.
